root_text.an10.72:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati mahāvane kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ sambahulehi abhiññātehi abhiññātehi therehi sāvakehi saddhiṁ—
root_text.an10.72:1.2: āyasmatā ca cālena, āyasmatā ca upacālena, āyasmatā ca kukkuṭena, āyasmatā ca kaḷimbhena, āyasmatā ca nikaṭena, āyasmatā ca kaṭissahena;
root_text.an10.72:1.3: aññehi ca abhiññātehi abhiññātehi therehi sāvakehi saddhiṁ.
root_text.an10.72:2.1: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā abhiññātā abhiññātā licchavī bhadrehi bhadrehi yānehi parapurāya uccāsaddā mahāsaddā mahāvanaṁ ajjhogāhanti bhagavantaṁ dassanāya.
root_text.an10.72:2.3: “ime kho sambahulā abhiññātā abhiññātā licchavī bhadrehi bhadrehi yānehi parapurāya uccāsaddā mahāsaddā mahāvanaṁ ajjhogāhanti bhagavantaṁ dassanāya.
root_text.an10.72:2.4: Saddakaṇṭakā kho pana jhānā vuttā bhagavatā.
root_text.an10.72:2.6: Tattha mayaṁ appasaddā appākiṇṇā phāsuṁ vihareyyāmā”ti.
root_text.an10.72:2.7: Atha kho te āyasmanto yena gosiṅgasālavanadāyo tenupasaṅkamiṁsu;
root_text.an10.72:2.8: tattha te āyasmanto appasaddā appākiṇṇā phāsuṁ viharanti.
root_text.an10.72:3.2: “kahaṁ nu kho, bhikkhave, cālo, kahaṁ upacālo, kahaṁ kukkuṭo, kahaṁ kaḷimbho, kahaṁ nikaṭo, kahaṁ kaṭissaho;
root_text.an10.72:3.3: kahaṁ nu kho te, bhikkhave, therā sāvakā gatā”ti?
root_text.an10.72:4.2: ‘ime kho sambahulā abhiññātā abhiññātā licchavī bhadrehi bhadrehi yānehi parapurāya uccāsaddā mahāsaddā mahāvanaṁ ajjhogāhanti bhagavantaṁ dassanāya saddakaṇṭakā kho pana jhānā vuttā bhagavatā yannūna mayaṁ yena gosiṅgasālavanadāyo tenupasaṅkameyyāma tattha mayaṁ appasaddā appākiṇṇā phāsuṁ vihareyyāmā’ti.
root_text.an10.72:4.3: Atha kho te, bhante, āyasmanto yena gosiṅgasālavanadāyo tenupasaṅkamiṁsu.
root_text.an10.72:4.4: Tattha te āyasmanto appasaddā appākiṇṇā phāsuṁ viharantī”ti.
root_text.an10.72:5.1: “Sādhu sādhu, bhikkhave, yathā te mahāsāvakā sammā byākaramānā byākareyyuṁ, saddakaṇṭakā hi, bhikkhave, jhānā vuttā mayā.
root_text.an10.72:6.3: Pavivekārāmassa saṅgaṇikārāmatā kaṇṭako, asubhanimittānuyogaṁ anuyuttassa subhanimittānuyogo kaṇṭako, indriyesu guttadvārassa visūkadassanaṁ kaṇṭako, brahmacariyassa mātugāmūpacāro kaṇṭako, paṭhamassa jhānassa saddo kaṇṭako, dutiyassa jhānassa vitakkavicārā kaṇṭakā, tatiyassa jhānassa pīti kaṇṭako, catutthassa jhānassa assāsapassāso kaṇṭako, saññāvedayitanirodhasamāpattiyā saññā ca vedanā ca kaṇṭako rāgo kaṇṭako doso kaṇṭako moho kaṇṭako.

translation_text.an10.72:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Vesālī, at the Great Wood, in the hall with the peaked roof, together with several well-known senior disciples.
translation_text.an10.72:1.2: They included Venerables Cāla, Upacāla, Kakkaṭa, Kaṭimbha, Kaṭa, Kaṭissaṅga,
translation_text.an10.72:2.1: Now at that time several well-known Licchavis plunged deep into the Great Wood to see the Buddha. Driving a succession of fine carriages, they made a colossal racket.
translation_text.an10.72:2.3: “These several well-known Licchavis have plunged deep into the Great Wood to see the Buddha. Driving a succession of fine carriages, they’re making a colossal racket.
translation_text.an10.72:2.4: But the Buddha has said that sound is a thorn to absorption.
translation_text.an10.72:2.5: Let’s go to the Gosiṅga Sal Wood.
translation_text.an10.72:2.6: There we can meditate comfortably, free of noise and crowds.”
translation_text.an10.72:2.7: Then those venerables went to the Gosiṅga Sal Wood,
translation_text.an10.72:2.8: where they meditated comfortably, free of noise and crowds.
translation_text.an10.72:3.1: Then the Buddha said to the mendicants:
translation_text.an10.72:3.2: “Mendicants, where are Cāla, Upacāla, Kakkaṭa, Kaṭimbha, Kaṭa, and Kaṭissaṅga?
translation_text.an10.72:3.3: Where have these senior disciples gone?”
translation_text.an10.72:4.1: And the mendicants told him what had happened.
translation_text.an10.72:5.1: “Good, good, mendicants! It’s just as those great disciples have so rightly explained. I have said that sound is a thorn to absorption.
translation_text.an10.72:6.1: Mendicants, there are these ten thorns.
translation_text.an10.72:6.3: Relishing company is a thorn for someone who loves seclusion. Focusing on the beautiful feature of things is a thorn for someone pursuing the meditation on ugliness. Seeing shows is a thorn to someone restraining the senses. Lingering in the neighborhood of ladies is a thorn to chastity. Sound is a thorn to the first absorption. Placing the mind and keeping it connected are a thorn to the second absorption. Rapture is a thorn to the third absorption. Breathing is a thorn to the fourth absorption. Perception and feeling are a thorn to the attainment of the cessation of perception and feeling. Greed, hate, and delusion are thorns.
translation_text.an10.72:7.3: Mendicants, live free of thorns and rid of thorns!
translation_text.an10.72:7.4: The perfected ones live free of thorns,

variant_text.an10.72:1.2: cālena → pālena (sya-all); cāḷena (pts1ed) | upacālena → upapālena (sya-all) | kukkuṭena → kakkaṭena (bj, sya-all, pts1ed) | kaḷimbhena → katimbhena (bj) | nikaṭena → kaṭena (bj)
variant_text.an10.72:6.3: brahmacariyassa → brahmacārissa (bj) | mātugāmūpacāro → mātugāmopavicāro (bj, pts1ed); mātugāmūpavicaro (mr)
